title: Jurinea macrocephala
intro: Jurinea macrocephala, also known as Jurinea or Kansuian, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of China and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects that can aid in reducing pain, swelling, and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Jurinea macrocephala include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Jurinea is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Jurinea macrocephala is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Jurinea, which includes a variety of plants native to the Mediterranean region and Central Asia. The genus Jurinea is further classified under the tribe Cardueae, and the family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the family Asteraceae, Jurinea macrocephala is also part of the order Asterales. The classification of Jurinea macrocephala reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.
uses: Jurinea macrocephala is primarily known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. Its root and flower extracts are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Research suggests that Jurinea macrocephala contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal benefits. In some parts of Asia, the plant is also used to treat digestive problems and as a natural remedy for colds and flu. Further studies are needed to fully understand the potential of Jurinea macrocephala as a medicinal plant.

parts: Jurinea macrocephala, the medicinal parts of this plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. The roots, stems, and flowers of Jurinea macrocephala are commonly used, with the roots being valued for their anti-inflammatory properties.
